commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi 3000GT Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi 3000GT's 6G72 engine family is robust when maintained, but three issues dominate UK replacement enquiries: timing belt failure on the interference-design 6G72, crankwalk bearing failure on 2G VR-4 models, and turbo seal degradation on high-mileage twin-turbo units. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address these original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Why Mitsubishi 3000GT 6G72 Engines Suffer Catastrophic Damage
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi 3000GT Base, SL, VR-4, GTO (1991-2001, all 6G72 variants)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-100,000 miles if belt not replaced per schedule
problems.rootCause: The 6G72 is an interference engine; if the timing belt snaps or jumps, pistons strike open valves causing bent valves, damaged pistons, or destroyed cylinder heads.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioners, water pump before failure
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic damage; recommended every 60k miles

repairOptions.tier: Major - Valve Repair After Belt Snap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, valve replacement, head skim, reassembly
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block undamaged; often not cost-effective

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 6G72 with upgraded components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when valves/pistons damaged; restores reliability

problems.recommendation: On a 3000GT worth £3,000-£8,000, preventative belt replacement is essential. If the belt has already failed, a rebuilt engine with upgraded internals typically offers better long-term value than attempting valve repairs on a 25+ year-old block.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 6G72 engine prices for your Mitsubishi 3000GT

problems.h4: Crankwalk Bearing Failure - Why 2G VR-4 Engines Develop Fatal End Play
problems.rootCause: Thrust bearing wear allows excessive crankshaft end play ("crankwalk"), causing clutch engagement issues, oil pressure loss, and eventual bearing seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removal, block machining, new thrust bearings, reassembly
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block not scored; requires specialist machining

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 6G72TT with upgraded crank bearings, balanced rotating assembly, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage or performance use; prevents recurrence

problems.recommendation: Crankwalk is a known weakness on 2G 6G72TT engines. If symptoms appear (clutch drag, oil pressure drop), replacement with a rebuilt unit featuring upgraded thrust bearings is the most reliable long-term solution for VR-4 owners.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 6G72TT crankwalk repair or replacement

problems.h4: Turbo Seal Failure - Why Mitsubishi 3000GT Twin Turbo Engines Lose Boost
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger oil seals degrade with heat and age, causing oil consumption, blue smoke, and loss of boost pressure; neglected turbos can send metal debris into the engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbos, replace seals or fit refurbished units, reassemble pipework
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine internals undamaged; requires specialist turbo knowledge

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 6G72TT with new or refurbished turbos, upgraded oil feed lines, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if turbo failure caused internal damage or for peace of mind

problems.recommendation: If boost loss is accompanied by metal debris in the intake or oil contamination, a full rebuilt engine with new turbos offers the most reliable outcome. For isolated turbo seal wear, specialist turbo refurbishment may suffice.
problems.cta: Compare Mitsubishi 3000GT twin turbo engine rebuild prices

problems.h4: Valve Stem Seal Wear - Why Mitsubishi 3000GT Engines Burn Oil
problems.rootCause: Rubber valve stem seals harden and crack with age, allowing oil to seep into combustion chambers, causing blue smoke on startup and increased oil consumption.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal or on-car seal replacement using specialised tools
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective fix if no other engine issues; restores oil control

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 6G72 with new seals, valve guides, and upgraded components
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if multiple issues present or for long-term reliability

problems.recommendation: Valve stem seal replacement is often the most economical fix for oil consumption on otherwise healthy 6G72 engines. However, if the engine has high mileage or other wear signs, a rebuilt unit may offer better value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi 3000GT oil consumption repair

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi 3000GT
finalCta.paragraph: Timing belt failure, crankwalk, turbo seal degradation, and valve stem wear are the most common reasons UK Mitsubishi 3000GT owners seek engine replacement. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists who understand the 6G72 family. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing these known failure points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get your 3000GT back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi 3000GT Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi 3000GT and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi 3000GT Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi 3000GT's 1991-2001 UK production run, organised so owners can quickly identify their 6G72 variant and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The 3000GT debuted with three 6G72 variants, establishing the SOHC/DOHC/TT hierarchy that defined the model's range.
keyChanges: UK launch of Mitsubishi 3000GT with Base, SL and VR-4 trims
Introduction of 6G72 SOHC (Base), DOHC (SL), and twin-turbo 6G72TT (VR-4)
All engines share 3.0L V6 architecture but differ in head design and aspiration
knownFor: Robust early 6G72 blocks with minimal crankwalk issues
Timing belt critical on all variants (interference design)
VR-4 twin turbos deliver strong performance but require meticulous maintenance
years.cta: Get quotes for 1991-1993 Mitsubishi 3000GT replacement engines
years.ticker: 3000GT VR-4 engine replacement • 6G72 timing belt failure • SL engine rebuild cost

years.description: The facelifted 3000GT introduced revised 6G72TT internals but also the notorious crankwalk issue on VR-4 and GTO models.
keyChanges: Introduction of GTO MR and Spyder variants alongside VR-4 and SL
Revised 6G72TT with updated turbochargers and engine management
Emergence of crankwalk (thrust bearing wear) as a known 2G 6G72TT weakness
knownFor: Crankwalk bearing failure risk on 6G72TT 1994-1997 models
Spyder convertible adds weight but retains DOHC engine
GTO MR features lightweight components and revised suspension
years.cta: Get quotes for 1994-1997 Mitsubishi 3000GT replacement engines
years.ticker: VR-4 crankwalk repair • 6G72TT engine rebuild • Spyder engine replacement cost

years.description: The final 3000GT models featured further 6G72TT refinements to address crankwalk, though the platform was discontinued in 2001.
keyChanges: Revised 6G72TT block with improved thrust bearing design
Updated ECU and emissions equipment for late-model compliance
knownFor: Later 6G72TT blocks less prone to crankwalk but not immune
High-mileage examples now require specialist rebuilding knowledge
Strong enthusiast community supports parts availability
years.cta: Get quotes for 1998-2001 Mitsubishi 3000GT replacement engines
years.ticker: Late VR-4 engine cost • 6G72TT turbo rebuild • 3000GT engine supply and fit

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Mitsubishi 3000GT was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Mitsubishi 3000GT specialists.

faq.h2: Mitsubishi 3000GT Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi 3000GT engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Mitsubishi 3000GT engines typically cost £1,500-£5,500 supply-only, depending on variant and engine code. Twin-turbo VR-4/GTO units command premium pricing due to complexity and specialist demand.
keyPoints: Base/SOHC rebuilt: £1,400-£2,800 supply only
SL/DOHC rebuilt: £1,500-£3,500 supply only
VR-4/6G72TT rebuilt: £2,500-£5,500 supply only
Supply & fit labour adds £800-£1,800 typically
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi 3000GT engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is it worth rebuilding a Mitsubishi 3000GT engine or replacing it?
items.answer: For timing belt failure or crankwalk damage, replacement with a rebuilt 6G72 usually offers better value than attempting repairs on a 25+ year-old block, especially with warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines include upgraded components addressing known failures
12-month warranty protects your investment vs used engine risk
Specialist rebuilders understand 6G72 nuances like crankwalk prevention
Consider vehicle value and intended use when deciding
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt Mitsubishi 3000GT engines with warranty

items.question: What causes Mitsubishi 3000GT crankwalk and can it be fixed?
items.answer: Crankwalk is thrust bearing wear allowing crankshaft end play, primarily affecting 1994-1997 6G72TT engines. Prevention requires upgraded bearings during rebuild.
keyPoints: Symptoms: clutch drag, oil pressure drop, knocking at idle
Caused by inadequate thrust bearing design on early 2G blocks
Repair requires block machining or full engine replacement
Rebuilt 6G72TT with upgraded bearings prevents recurrence
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi 3000GT VR-4 crankwalk repair or replacement quotes

items.question: How often should I replace the timing belt on a Mitsubishi 3000GT 6G72 engine?
items.answer: Replace the 6G72 timing belt every 60,000 miles or 5 years, whichever comes first. The interference design means belt failure causes bent valves and costly damage.
keyPoints: Use OEM-spec belt, tensioners and water pump together
Interference engine: belt snap = bent valves, damaged pistons
Labour-intensive job; budget £500-£900 specialist, £800-£1,400 dealer
Always verify belt history when buying a used 3000GT
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi 3000GT timing belt replacement or engine rebuild costs

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Mitsubishi 3000GT?
items.answer: While swaps like the 6G74 3.5L are technically possible, most UK owners opt for correct-spec 6G72 replacement to maintain compatibility with ECU, wiring and emissions systems.
keyPoints: 6G72 variants (SOHC/DOHC/TT) are direct replacements within model years
6G74 3.5L swaps require extensive modification and tuning
ECU, wiring loom and exhaust compatibility critical for OBD1/OBD2 models
Specialist fitment recommended for any non-standard engine
items.cta: Get quotes for correct-spec Mitsubishi 3000GT engine replacement

items.question: Where can I find a reliable Mitsubishi 3000GT engine supplier in the UK?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK specialists who rebuild 6G72 engines with upgraded components and offer 12-month warranties, avoiding the risk of untested used engines.
keyPoints: Network includes 100+ Mitsubishi-specialist rebuilders and suppliers
All quotes include warranty terms, condition and delivery options
Compare supply-only vs supply & fit pricing side-by-side
DVLA registration lookup ensures correct engine code match
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi 3000GT engine suppliers and quotes instantly

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned, rebuilt and remanufactured 3000GT engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned engines replace worn parts; rebuilt engines include upgrades addressing known failures; remanufactured units meet factory-spec standards like BS AU 257 with full component renewal.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: good for budget daily drivers, 12-month warranty typical
Rebuilt: upgraded internals for performance or high-mileage use
Remanufactured: factory-spec rebuild, longest warranty, premium pricing
Always confirm scope of work and warranty terms before ordering
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi 3000GT reconditioned vs rebuilt engine options

items.question: How long does a Mitsubishi 3000GT engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch in 3-10 working days; supply & fit installations require 2-4 days garage time depending on variant complexity and parts availability.
keyPoints: Used engines may be available immediately if in stock
Rebuilt/remanufactured units built to order: allow 1-3 weeks
Twin-turbo VR-4/GTO installations take longer due to complex pipework
Mobile fitters available in some regions for on-site replacement
items.cta: Book Mitsubishi 3000GT engine supply & fit with UK specialists

items.question: Do Mitsubishi 3000GT engines last?
items.answer: Well-maintained 6G72 engines can exceed 150,000 miles, but timing belt neglect, crankwalk on 2G TT models, and valve seal wear are common failure points requiring proactive management.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes and timing belt replacement critical for longevity
VR-4/GTO TT engines demand higher maintenance standards
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components often outlast original spec
Service history verification essential when buying used
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi 3000GT engines with proven reliability and warranty

items.question: Will a rebuilt Mitsubishi 3000GT engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes - every engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from the rebuilder, protecting your investment against premature failure.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Terms vary by supplier; always confirm before ordering
Used engines may have shorter or conditional warranties
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi 3000GT engine warranty terms from UK specialists
